purpose: Compare any set of options (frameworks, vendors, tools, technologies) across consistent criteria in a single table.
primaryUserGoal: Pick the best option from a shortlist by scanning how each performs across the criteria that matter.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to compare vendors, tools, frameworks, or technologies — anywhere the decision is 'X vs Y vs Z across these dimensions'.
goodFor: Framework comparisons (React vs Vue vs Svelte)
supports: Stats with summary counts (options, criteria, winner)
Comparison-table with vendor logos via leadingImage
Tone-coded cells: success for best, warning for caveat, danger for gap
Notes for context and weighting rationale
idealDataShape: Each cell is a short value with optional tone

populationInstructions.summary: Lead with stats (options, criteria, recommended winner). Then a comparison-table: rows are vendors (with leadingImage logo, query '<vendor> logo'), columns are criteria (price, features, support, integration, etc.). Notes at the bottom for qualitative context.
steps: Identify the class of thing being compared (analytics platforms, frontend frameworks, etc.).
For each vendor, populate the row with short cell values. Use subvalue for details.
Mark the best cell per column with tone='success' and emphasis=true.
Use tone='warning' for notable caveats, 'danger' for gaps or unsupported.
Include a leadingImage query like '<vendor> logo'.
Summarize the recommendation in the notes section.
guardrails: Never invent prices or support SLAs — leave as 'unknown' if not verified.
Keep cell values short — put nuance in notes.
Don't use more than 6 columns; past that readability collapses.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_table_rows to add or refresh a vendor
Use set_scope_tags for scope chips (region, company size)
